---
date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY/MM/DD"
title: "29. Proportions of the Normal Curve"
subtitle: "One-Tailed Reference Table and Reproducible Generator"
reference-location: margin
resources:
  - ../../tables/make_normal_tail_table.R
  - ../../tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.csv
  - ../../tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.tex
  - ../../tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.pdf
---

This page reproduces the classic one-tailed normal table. Each cell gives the
right-tail probability

$$P(Z \ge z)$$

for a standard normal deviate, where the row gives the first decimal place and
the column gives the second decimal place. For example, the entry at row `1.5`
and column `1` corresponds to $z = 1.51$ and gives `0.0655`.

The source files are available here:

- [R script](../../tables/make_normal_tail_table.R)
- [CSV values](../../tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.csv)
- [LaTeX source](../../tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.tex)
- [PDF table](../../tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.pdf)

The generated table files are committed and published as static resources, so
they are not rebuilt during routine site renders. To regenerate them
explicitly, run:

```bash
Rscript tables/make_normal_tail_table.R
```

The PDF below is produced by the R script and typeset in LaTeX.

```{=html}
<object data="../../tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.pdf" type="application/pdf" width="100%" height="1600">
  <p>The embedded PDF could not be displayed here. Use the download links above.</p>
</object>
```
